UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile in the dating app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile.

location.description: User's current location (e.g., coordinates or address).

Match.title: Match
Match.description: Represents a match between two users.

user1Id.description: Reference to the first user in the match. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Match)

user2Id.description: Reference to the second user in the match. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Match)

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents a chat message between two users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the chat message.

senderId.description: Reference to the user who sent the message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage)

receiverId.description: Reference to the user who received the message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage)

matchId.description: Reference to the match associated with the message. (Relationship: Match 1:N ChatMessage)

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the message was sent.

Like.title: Like
Like.description: Represents a user's like or dislike of another user.

likerId.description: Reference to the user who is liking. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Like)

likeeId.description: Reference to the user who is being liked. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Like)

isLike.description: Indicates whether the user liked (true) or disliked (false) the other user.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the like/dislike was recorded.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores user likes/dislikes. Path-based ownership, only the user can manage their likes.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who is liking.

params.name: likeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the like.

definition.description: Stores match data between two users. Includes denormalized 'user1Id' and 'user2Id' for authorization independence.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier for the match.

definition.description: Stores chat messages between matched users. Authorization is based on membership in the match, which is validated using the denormalized 'user1Id' and 'user2Id' in the /matches/{matchId} document.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier for the match.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the core features of the GoldChic Dating app, focusing on user profiles, matching, likes, and real-time chat. The design emphasizes authorization independence and efficient data retrieval, adhering to the core design principles.

*   **UserProfile:** User profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`. This path-based ownership ensures that only the user can read and write their profile data. This approach naturally supports QAPs as only the user can query their own data.
*   **Likes:** Likes/dislikes are stored in `/users/{userId}/likes/{likeId}`. This structure uses path-based ownership for managing likes. A user can only create, update, or delete likes associated with their own profile. The `likeeId` is stored within the like document.  Queries for likes given and received are possible using collection group queries (though potentially expensive at scale).
*   **Matches:** Matches are stored in a top-level `/matches/{matchId}` collection. Each match document contains `user1Id` and `user2Id` fields.  To ensure authorization independence, the members of the match (`user1Id`, `user2Id`) are copied to each document. This design enables rules to verify if the requesting user is either `user1Id` or `user2Id` without additional `get()` calls. This addresses authorization independence and supports listing matches for a user without filtering.
*   **Chat Messages:** Chat messages are stored in `/matches/{matchId}/messages/{messageId}`.  Authorization depends on the user being a member of the match. The `matchId` links to the match document, and because the match document denormalizes the `user1Id` and `user2Id`, security rules can validate that the user is authorized to view or send messages within that match.

This structure promotes security, scalability, and debuggability by minimizing hierarchical dependencies and clearly defining ownership and access rights.
